title: heptapleurum exaltatum
intro: Heptapleurum exaltatum, also known as the East Indian arrowroot, is a plant species in the family Araliaceae. The East Indian arrowroot has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and fever, as well as its use as a diuretic and a laxative. The plant's roots and rhizomes are rich in starch and are believed to have health benefits such as lowering blood pressure and improving digestion. In herbal preparations, Heptapleurum exaltatum is often made into a decoction or a powder, which can be used to treat various ailments, including gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions.
